critic_findings.message: _get_state_dir() falls back to Path('') when STATE_DIR is unset. Path('') resolves to the process cwd at call time — silently scanning the wrong directory with no diagnostic. Fallback should raise RuntimeError('STATE_DIR env var is not set').

test_main_reads_state_dir_from_env does not verify per-invocation reading. It sets STATE_DIR once and calls main() once — identical to every other test. Must call main() twice with different STATE_DIR values via monkeypatch.setenv to prove the env var is read at call time, not at import.
Spec checklist item 3 requires a test for 'No subcommand -> stderr ERROR: missing subcommand + exit 1'. No test covers main([]) in either test_state_sync_ops_cli.py or test_state_sync_shim.py.

_make_state_dirs() and _write_fixture() are defined identically in test_state_sync_ops.py:17-28, test_state_sync_ops_cli.py:16-27, and _make_state_dirs alone in test_state_sync_shim.py:19. Move both to engineer/scripts/conftest.py as pytest fixtures.
